nist.description: Map NIST 800-53 controls to AWS service capabilities
nist.prompt_template:
Your task is to map NIST 800-53 controls (revision 5) to {AWS-Service} capabilities. You should do the following steps:
1. List all NIST controls that are applicable to {AWS-Service}
2. For each control use AWS documentation and find relevant {AWS-Service} capability
3. You need to be detailed. Avoid using generic capabilities like "IAM". For example:
   Control: Least privilege
   {AWS-Service} capability: EC2 machine must use instance profiles with and associated AWS role with minimum privileges
   Another example
   Control: Data encryption
   {AWS-Service} capability: EBS storage must be encrypted with KMS keys
4. Sort the controls by criticality, with "Baseline" controls first, followed by "Optional" controls
5. Only include the top 20 most critical controls - do NOT include any controls that are not applicable to {AWS-Service}
6. For each control rate the control in one of the following:
   a. Baseline (must have)
   b. Optional (for security high bar)
7. For each control categorise it into:
   a. Infrastructure protection
   b. Data protection
   c. Identity & Access Management
   d. Incident Response
   e. Logging & monitoring
   f. Application protection

IMPORTANT: You MUST return your response as a valid JSON array using EXACTLY these field names:
[
  {
    "id": "control ID and name",
    "category": "category name",
    "priority": "Baseline or Optional",
    "description": "detailed AWS service capability"
  }
]

Return ONLY the JSON array with no additional text, markdown, or formatting.

iso27001.description: Map ISO 27001 Annex A controls to AWS service capabilities
iso27001.prompt_template:

   {AWS-Service} capability: VPC security groups must restrict inbound traffic to necessary ports and sources only

6. For each control rate the control in one of the following:

   c. Human resource security

   e. Access control
   f. Cryptography
   g. Physical and environmental security
   h. Operations security
   i. Communications security
   j. System acquisition, development and maintenance
   k. Supplier relationships

Return ONLY the JSON array with no additional text, markdown, or formatting.
